message.content:
Adversarially verify this finding about a design doc (the doc is at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-jleechan-projects-worktree-runner/8ca589dd-8422-44b1-a543-52bd7b71f14b/scratchpad/gistfile1.txt; the repo is /home/jleechan/projects/worktree_runner; you may use WebSearch/WebFetch and read files). Try to REFUTE it. Finding from the "rust" reviewer, severity critical:
TITLE: Module table promises 7 files; main.rs declares 4 — service.rs and limits.rs don't exist and `install-service` silently succeeds while doing nothing
DETAIL: Section 4 (lines 62–78) lists src/service.rs and src/limits.rs as core modules, but main.rs (lines 101–104) declares only `mod platform; mod backend; mod config; mod runner;`. Additionally `Commands::InstallService` (lines 147–149) prints 'Service installation not yet implemented' and returns Ok(()) — exit code 0 for an unimplemented operation. Two failure scenarios: (1) anyone implementing from this doc will discover mid-build that the design/skeleton contract is broken and improvise the service/limits layer without a spec; (2) scripts doing `gha-isolated install-service && systemctl start ...` will proceed as if the service were installed. Note also that 'Resource Limits' is a headline core principle (line 11) yet limits.rs — the module that would own it — is one of the two missing files.
RECOMMENDATION: Either add service.rs/limits.rs stubs so the skeleton matches the table, or cut the table to 5 files and move service/limits to an explicit v1.1 section. Make every unimplemented subcommand `anyhow::bail!("not implemented")` (non-zero exit), never println + Ok.
Is the finding factually correct and does the recommendation make sense? If the factual basis is wrong or the severity is inflated, refute it. Default to refuted=true only if you have concrete contrary evidence or the claim is unsupported after checking.

content.text: The doc's factual claims check out at the cited lines. Let me check whether the repo itself contains any gha-isolated implementation that would bear on this.

input.command: rg -l --hidden -g '!.git' 'gha-isolated|gha_isolated' /home/jleechan/projects/worktree_runner | head -20; echo "---"; find /home/jleechan/projects/worktree_runner -name 'main.rs' -o -name 'Cargo.toml' 2>/dev/null | grep -v '.git' | head
input.description: Search repo for gha-isolated implementation or Rust files

input.reasoning: Every factual citation in the finding is accurate: the Section 4 table (doc lines 62-78) lists 7 files including src/service.rs and src/limits.rs; main.rs (lines 101-104) declares only 4 modules; InstallService (lines 147-149) prints a message and returns Ok(()) with exit code 0; line 11 lists resource protection as a core principle. However, the finding is refuted on severity and framing: (1) the doc discloses the gap twice — line 283 marks install-service "(future)" and Next Steps item 2 explicitly lists service installation as unbuilt — so the "broken contract discovered mid-build" scenario is contradicted by the doc's own text; (2) "silently succeeds" is wrong — it prints "not yet implemented", and the identical println+Ok(()) placeholder pattern is used by start/stop/status too (runner.rs is all placeholders), so this is skeleton-wide style, not a unique defect; (3) "limits.rs missing leaves Resource Limits with no owner" is overstated — config.rs generates memory/cpus/pids_limit inline for both backend paths; (4) no code exists in the repo (rg/find confirm zero Rust files), so this is a proposal doc with no deployment surface — "critical" severity presumes production scripting of code the doc labels future. The residual valid core is a low/medium doc-consistency issue: the 7-file table vs 4-module skeleton mismatch clashes with the doc's "Full Self-Contained" / "runnable today" claims. The recommendation (trim table or add stubs; bail! on unimplemented subcommands) is sound engineering advice worth adopting if the doc becomes an implementation contract.
input.corrected_claim: Severity low/medium (doc consistency), not critical: the Section 4 module table lists src/service.rs and src/limits.rs but the skeleton's main.rs declares only platform/backend/config/runner, which clashes with the doc's "Full Self-Contained" and "runnable today" claims. However, the service gap is explicitly disclosed twice (usage comment "Install as service (future)" and Next Steps item 2), install-service prints "not yet implemented" rather than failing silently, the same placeholder println+Ok(()) pattern applies to start/stop/status as well, and resource limits are already owned by config.rs inline. Recommended fix stands: reconcile the table with the skeleton (stubs or trim to 4-5 files with an explicit future-work section) and make unimplemented subcommands exit non-zero via anyhow::bail!.
